title: Server Page Components
text: Server Page Components Server page components provide the user interface for web applications. Uniface provides two types of components for server pages. Dynamic Server Pages DSPs support client-side code execution so you can use JavaScript to define behavior that is only relevant in the browser and which does not involve data I/O or server-side processing. They also provide a more extensive and flexible set of widgets than the basic HTML controls used in static server pages. DSP components are split into a non-persistent part that runs on the server, and a persistent part that runs on the browser. Communication between the two parts is asynchronous, via HTTP requests and responses. Uniface automatically handles communication and synchronization. For more information, see Dynamic Server Pages . Static Server Pages Static Server Page (USP) components generate a complete web page very time they are activated. This makes them useful for relatively static web pages or a thin-client architecture, in which all processing is done on the server. The client is only used to consume and/or display HTML content. While it is possible to make static pages more dynamic by adding your own JavaScript, it does not provide 'out-of-the-box' support, which the dynamic server page does. All interaction passes through the server—data is sent to the server, the server responds, and the entire HTML page is reloaded with the response. Communication between the client and server is synchronous, so the client has to wait until it receives a response from the server. As a result, much more data is sent back and forth between the client and server, and user interactions are much less dynamic. USPs can only be used in web applications, and are not suitable for mobile apps. For more information, see Static Server Pages . Data Structure and Layout Server page definitions consist of a component structure and a web layout. The component structure defines the data content of the page. The web layout determines the arrangement and order of fields, the type of controls used to display and update the data, and the general look and feel of the page. The component structure and layout are bound together at runtime to generate the web page that is sent to the browser. For more information, see Binding Web Layout to Component Structure . By default, the layout is internal to the server page component, but it is possible to store it as one or more separate files with an .hts extension. Uniface can then be instructed to use the external file at runtime. The binding information in the layout definition ensures that it can be bound to the component data structure. For more information, see External Web Layout Definitions . Related Links Presentation-Logic Split for Server Pages The data structure and layout definitions of all server pages can be defined and stored separately. Binding Web Layout to Component Structure Binding refers to the way in which layout elements are connected to data structures and triggers in the component structure. It ensures that data is displayed by the appropriate HTML elements in the web page, and that the correct trigger is executed when data in a field is changed. Dynamic Server Page A Dynamic Server Page is a Component that defines a web page that can be incrementally and dynamically updated at runtime. Static Server Page A Static Server Page is a Component that defines a web page that is updated as a complete page at runtime. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
